meta.siteDescription: Burak Boduroglu's personal site focused on context engineering, prompt engineering, agentic coding, and production-minded fullstack/DevOps work.

home.eyebrow: Context Engineering | Agentic Coding | Prompt Engineering
home.heroSubtitle: I build applications and automation solutions.
home.sub: I build web applications and automation solutions with a strong focus on context engineering and prompt engineering. My core stack includes Next.js, Node.js, TypeScript, Java/Spring Boot, PostgreSQL, n8n, and container-based deployments with Docker/Podman on VPS environments.
home.pillarFullstackDesc: I design TypeScript apps with APIs and data layers that are production-ready from the beginning.
home.pillarAutomationDesc: I automate repetitive work and make integrations observable with n8n-style orchestration.
home.pillarDevopsDesc: I work with container-based environments, VPS, and Dokploy for safer deploys and stable operations.
home.pillarAiDesc: I build applications and automation solutions with Context & Prompt Engineering.

about.title: About
about.metaDescription: Burak Boduroglu  -  context engineering, prompt engineering, agentic coding, fullstack development, and DevOps.
about.lead: "To me, quality software means a system where context, prompts, and engineering discipline work together."
about.p1: I build products with Next.js and TypeScript; on the backend I ship services with Node.js and Java (Spring Boot), and I design data layers with PostgreSQL. For integration workflows I use n8n, and for delivery I use Docker/Podman with VPS/Dokploy.
about.p2: Across my projects, I handle LLM usage with context engineering and prompt engineering practices. I also actively use tools like Cursor, Codex, and Claude Code to move faster with clearer quality control.

approach.lead: Clarify the problem, constraints, and success criteria first
approach.detail: Starting to build without knowing what you're shipping is like sailing without a compass.

approach.lead: Small, repeatable deliveries and early feedback loops
approach.detail: Prefer frequent, small steps over a big bang - the earlier you catch issues, the cheaper they are to fix.
You need to see what's happening inside the system - and get back to a last-known-good state quickly when things break.

approach.lead: On the LLM layer: context engineering, prompt engineering, and guardrail-led evaluation loops
approach.detail: What the model sees, what it should do, and where boundaries are drawn should be tested continuously.

approach.lead: In agentic flows, define task breakdown, tool choice, and quality gates upfront
approach.detail: Before an autonomous agent runs, it should be clear which step it takes, which tool it uses, and how outputs are validated.
